mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in real GDP and retail sales indicate a broad demand shock that forces earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.1 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, driving positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases do not precede positive 5-day returns, with a directional accuracy below 45% in a 2-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and forces equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand that supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a cumulative alpha below zero over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal strengthening residential demand and construction activity that boosts related sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not precede positive 60-day returns, with a directional accuracy below 48% across all historical expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply bottlenecks and capacity constraints that trigger short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions below 100 fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a win rate below 40% in late-cycle regimes over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
